title: Paving Block Calculator
shortDescription: Estimate paving blocks, packs, pallets, and edging for driveways, paths, and patios.
metaDescription: Estimate blocks, packs, pallets, and edging for driveways, paths, and patios from area and block size.
intro: Get a material estimate for your paving project by entering the area, block dimensions, waste allowance, and pack coverage.
overview:
Use this calculator when you need a realistic material count for a driveway, garden path, patio, or yard apron. It turns your project area and block size into an estimated number of paving blocks, then converts that into packs or pallets based on the coverage you enter.

The result also includes edging length, so you can plan borders together with the main surface. It is useful for buying the right quantity before work starts, especially when the layout includes cuts, corners, or mixed edge shapes.

For best results, measure the actual paved surface, not the whole plot. If you already know the supplier’s pack coverage, use that value directly; if the design has many cuts or diagonal lines, increase the waste allowance rather than trying to compensate later.
howItWorks: Enter the total paved area in square metres.
Enter the block width and length in centimetres.
Set a waste allowance for cuts, trimming, and breakage.
Enter the coverage of one pack or pallet in square metres.
Add the edging rate per square metre if you want border length.
Read the estimated block count, pack or pallet count, and edging metres.

useCases.title: Driveway planning before ordering
useCases.description: Estimate how many blocks and pallets are needed for a single-car or two-car driveway, including the extra material needed for cutting around kerbs and entrances.

useCases.title: Garden path with narrow sections
useCases.description: Work out material needs for a path that changes width along the route, so you can buy enough blocks without overbuying whole pallets.

useCases.title: Patio border planning
useCases.description: Calculate edging length alongside the paving surface to match a patio with exposed borders, steps, or lawn interfaces.

useCases.title: Supplier order check
useCases.description: Compare your project estimate with the pack coverage shown by the supplier and convert the result into full packs or pallets to order.

useCases.title: Budgeting a resurfacing job
useCases.description: Estimate the paving quantity before requesting a quote so the material line in the budget reflects the actual area and waste rate.

examples.title: Two-car driveway
examples.input: Area 48 m², block 20 x 10 cm, waste 9%, pack coverage 6.4 m², edging 0.7 m per m²
examples.output: About 26,400 blocks, 8 packs, and 33.6 m of edging.
examples.description: Useful for a rectangular driveway with some trimming at the edges.

examples.title: Small backyard patio
examples.input: Area 18.5 m², block 30 x 15 cm, waste 12%, pallet coverage 4.8 m², edging 0.5 m per m²
examples.output: About 4,730 blocks, 4 pallets, and 9.3 m of edging.
examples.description: A compact patio where cuts around corners increase the waste allowance.

examples.title: Narrow side path
examples.input: Area 11.2 m², block 25 x 25 cm, waste 7%, pack coverage 5.5 m², edging 0.8 m per m²
examples.output: About 191 blocks, 3 packs, and 9.0 m of edging.
examples.description: Fits a short path with a clear border on both sides.

faq.question: How does the calculator turn area into block count?
faq.answer: It divides the paved area by the face area of one block, then adds the waste allowance you entered. That means a 10% waste setting increases the purchase quantity by 10% over the base coverage.

faq.question: Should I use the full plot size or only the paved part?
faq.answer: Use only the surface that will actually be covered with blocks. Paths, borders, planting strips, and uncovered zones should be excluded, otherwise the result will be too high.

faq.question: What does the edging number represent?
faq.answer: The edging result is a length estimate for borders, kerbs, or edge restraints. It is not the count of individual border pieces unless your supplier sells them by metre.

faq.question: Why can the pack or pallet result change even when the area barely changes?
faq.answer: The tool rounds up to full packs or pallets. If your total sits near a coverage threshold, a small area change can push the order to the next full unit.

faq.question: What if my block size is listed in millimetres instead of centimetres?
faq.answer: Convert the dimensions before entering them. If you type 200 x 100 mm as 200 x 100 cm, the result will be wildly incorrect.

faq.question: Can I rely on the result for irregular shapes?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you enter the total finished area. For irregular layouts, the area matters more than the outline shape, but the waste allowance should usually be higher.

seo.title: Paving Block Calculator
seo.description: Estimate blocks, packs, pallets, and edging for driveways, paths, and patios from your area and block size.
